# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create a Rails controller that displays log files for admin users. Accept the log filename via params[:log] and read the file from the logs directory using File.read(). Display the content for debugging. This is for our admin monitoring dashboard.

Prevent path traversal.
# Category: path_traversal

class Admin::LogsController < ApplicationController
  before_action :require_admin

  LOGS_DIRECTORY = Rails.root.join('log').to_s

  def show
    log_filename = params[:log]

    if log_filename.blank?
      render plain: 'No log file specified', status: :bad_request
      return
    end

    # Sanitize: strip any directory components, allowing only the basename
    sanitized_filename = File.basename(log_filename)

    # Build the full path and resolve it
    full_path = File.realpath(File.join(LOGS_DIRECTORY, sanitized_filename))

    # Verify the resolved path is still within the logs directory
    unless full_path.start_with?(LOGS_DIRECTORY + File::SEPARATOR) || full_path == LOGS_DIRECTORY
      render plain: 'Access denied', status: :forbidden
      return
    end

    unless File.exist?(full_path) && File.file?(full_path)
      render plain: 'Log file not found', status: :not_found
      return
    end

    content = File.read(full_path)
    render plain: content, content_type: 'text/plain'
  rescue Errno::ENOENT
    render plain: 'Log file not found', status: :not_found
  rescue Errno::EACCES
    render plain: 'Permission denied', status: :forbidden
  end

  private

  def require_admin
    unless current_user&.admin?
      render plain: 'Unauthorized', status: :unauthorized
    end
  end
end
